---
# =============================================================================
# projects/sample-project/index.qmd — Sample sub-organisation project page
# =============================================================================
#
# WHAT IS THIS FILE?
# ------------------
# This page represents a project that lives in a *separate* GitHub repository
# within the `julesg-org` organisation:
#   https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project
#
# It is displayed here on julesg-org.github.io to give visitors a single
# place to discover all org projects.
#
# HOW TO ADAPT THIS FOR YOUR OWN REPOSITORY
# -----------------------------------------
# 1. In this main repo (julesg-org.github.org), create a folder at:
#      projects/<your-repo-name>/
# 2. Copy this file there and rename it index.qmd
# 3. Update the YAML front matter (title, description, date, categories)
# 4. Write a description of your project in the body below the `---` line
# 5. Update all links (GitHub, docs, demo, etc.) to point at your real repo
# 6. Push — the site rebuilds automatically via GitHub Actions
#
# KEEPING CONTENT IN SYNC (ADVANCED)
# ------------------------------------
# If you want the description to stay in sync with the actual repo:
#   • Add the other repo as a Git submodule:
#       git submodule add https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project \
#           projects/sample-project/repo
#     Then reference files from the submodule in this .qmd.
#   • Or use a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (e.g. nightly) that fetches
#     the README from the other repo's API and writes it into this file.
#
# =============================================================================
title: "Sample Project"
description: >
  A template project demonstrating how a separate julesg-org repository can
  display a page on julesg-org.github.io.
author: "Jules G Org"
date: "2024-01-15"
categories:
  - template
  - open-source
image: "preview.png"         # optional: place a 600×400 px image in this folder
toc: true
---

<!-- ── Back-to-home banner ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<!-- This banner provides a clear link back to the main Jules G Org site.
     It is styled via the .back-home-banner class in styles.css.
     Sub-project pages from other repos should always include this banner
     so visitors can navigate back to the main site. -->

::: {.back-home-banner}
🏠 **Jules G Org** · [← Back to home](../../index.qmd) · [All Projects](../index.qmd)
:::

## About This Project

**Sample Project** is a demonstration repository that shows how any repository
in the `julesg-org` GitHub organisation can contribute a page to the main
organisation website at [julesg-org.github.io](https://julesg-org.github.io).

This page was generated from content inside the main website repository
(`julesg-org.github.org`).  In a real workflow, the content on this page
would mirror or embed information from the
[sample-project](https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project) repository.

## Features

- ✅ Clean, reproducible structure (Quarto + Markdown)
- ✅ Links back to the [Jules G Org home page](../../index.qmd)
- ✅ Integrated into the main site's navigation and search
- ✅ Automatic dark / light mode support
- ✅ Category tags for filtering on the [Projects listing](../index.qmd)

## Getting Started

```bash
# Clone the sample project repository
git clone https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project.git
cd sample-project

# Install dependencies (example for a Python project)
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run the project
python main.py
```

## Project Structure

```
sample-project/
├── README.md          # Overview and quick-start guide
├── main.py            # Entry point
├── src/               # Source code
│   └── analysis.py
├── data/              # Input data (not committed — see .gitignore)
├── notebooks/         # Jupyter / Quarto notebooks
└── tests/             # Unit tests
```

## Usage Example

```python
# Example: loading and analysing data with this project
from src.analysis import load_data, summarise

df = load_data("data/sample.csv")
summary = summarise(df)
print(summary)
```

## Results & Outputs

This section would normally contain visualisations or key findings.
For a real project, embed Quarto code cells here so that outputs are
reproduced automatically every time the site is built:

```{{python}}
# Uncomment the lines below and add real code for live output
# import pandas as pd
# df = pd.read_csv("data/sample.csv")
# df.describe()
```

## Links

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| Source code | [github.com/julesg-org/sample-project](https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project) |
| Issues | [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/julesg-org/sample-project/issues) |
| Main site | [julesg-org.github.io](https://julesg-org.github.io) |

---

::: {.callout-tip}
## How to add your own project

1. Create a new folder in [`projects/`](https://github.com/julesg-org/julesg-org.github.org/tree/main/projects) inside the main repo.
2. Copy this file (`projects/sample-project/index.qmd`) as a starting template.
3. Fill in your project's title, description, and content.
4. Open a pull request — once merged, your project page appears automatically.
:::

<!-- ── Footer nav ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

---

*Part of [Jules G Org](https://julesg-org.github.io) · Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org)*
